reasoning: The market estimate is at 0.65 as of April 2, 2026. Recent outbreak data shows heavy case volumes in 2025 (2,286 cases) and 2026 (1,714 cases by April). Of the 2026 cases, 1,232 are linked to outbreaks that started in 2025, which gives strong epidemiological support for continuous transmission lasting over 12 months. However, the largest single outbreak (West Texas) officially ended in August 2025, which means continuity depends on other chains. Furthermore, genomic sequencing is struggling with low resolution due to only two genotypes (B3 and B8) circulating, which might give PAHO plausibility to determine multiple separate importations rather than an endemic continuous chain, similar to how they ruled in 2019. The PAHO review meeting is scheduled for November 2026, creating a tight timeline for an official declaration before 2027. Given these conflicting factors (high likelihood of 12-month epidemiological linkage vs. rigid genomic evidence standards and administrative delays), the probability remains close to the community estimate of 65%.
